flashcards.Back: A Layer 2 security feature that acts as a firewall between untrusted hosts and trusted DHCP servers, preventing rogue DHCP server attacks.
A database built by DHCP snooping that tracks the MAC address, assigned IP, lease time, and port for all clients on untrusted interfaces.
A port connected to a legitimate DHCP server or a relay agent. it is allowed to send all types of DHCP messages, including offers and acknowledgments.
An access port where end-user devices are connected. DHCP snooping blocks any DHCP server-side messages (like Offer or ACK) from these ports.
A security feature that validates ARP packets in a network by comparing them against the DHCP snooping binding table to prevent ARP spoofing.
A malicious technique where an attacker sends forged ARP messages to associate their MAC address with the IP address of a legitimate gateway or server.
A Cisco switch feature that limits the number of MAC addresses allowed on a single port, providing control over which devices can connect to the network.
A port security feature that dynamically learns a MAC address and saves it permanently to the running configuration so it persists across reboots.
The default port security violation mode. it immediately disables the port (err-disabled state), stops all traffic, and sends a log message.
A violation mode that drops unauthorized traffic, increments a counter, and generates a syslog/SNMP notification, but keeps the port operational.
The least restrictive violation mode. it silently drops unauthorized traffic without generating any logs or disabling the port.
A port status indicating it has been administratively shut down by the system due to a security violation like port security or BPDU guard.
A statistic in port security that tracks the number of times unauthorized MAC addresses have attempted to access a secured port.
A protection mechanism in Dynamic ARP Inspection that limits the number of ARP packets allowed per second to prevent CPU exhaustion.
A manually configured list used by DAI to validate ARP packets for hosts that use static IP addresses and are not in the DHCP snooping table.
A security feature that uses the DHCP snooping table to verify that an incoming packet's source IP matches the MAC address assigned to that port.
A type of ARP packet often used by attackers to update the ARP caches of other devices. DAI is used to validate and filter these messages.
An authorized hardware address that is manually entered by an administrator into the port security configuration for a specific interface.
The command parameter used to define how many unique MAC addresses are allowed to be learned or configured on a secured interface.
A switch port connected to another switch or a router where ARP packets are not inspected, as those devices are considered trustworthy.
The mandatory command used to enable the port security feature on a Cisco interface once it has been configured as an access port.
The global command used to specify which VLANs should be monitored by the DHCP snooping feature.
The verification command that displays the status, violation mode, and learned MAC addresses for a specific secured port.
A verification command that shows the DAI status and statistics for a particular VLAN, including the number of dropped packets.
A global setting that allows a switch to automatically re-enable an err-disabled port after a specified amount of time.
A denial-of-service attack where an actor exhausts the DHCP server's address pool using forged MAC addresses. Port security mitigates this.
An attack where thousands of fake MAC addresses are sent to a switch to fill its MAC table, forcing it to act as a hub. Port security prevents this.
A field in DHCP packets where a switch can insert its own ID and port information to help the server assign IPs based on physical location.
A feature that allows a switch to save its DHCP snooping table to an external file so it is not lost during a power failure or reboot.
An access port where all incoming ARP packets are intercepted and checked against the binding table for validity.
